basics.name: Junteng Liu
basics.label: Ph.D. Candidate in Computer Science
basics.summary: Ph.D. candidate at the HKUST NLP Group (Hong Kong University of Science and Technology), advised by Prof. Junxian He. Previously completed a B.Eng. in Automation (IEEE Honor Class) at Shanghai Jiao Tong University (SJTU), awarded the Zhiyuan Honor Scholarship. Research interests span large language model reasoning, agentic LLMs, vision-language models, and LLM truthfulness and interpretability.

work.summary: Worked on large-scale logical reasoning data synthesis and training for LLMs.
Studied perception bottlenecks in vision-language models for chart understanding.
Worked on LLM truthfulness and internal representation analysis.

education.institution: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

publications.name: SynLogic: Synthesizing Verifiable Reasoning Data at Scale for Learning Logical Reasoning and Beyond
publications.summary: A framework for large-scale synthesis of verifiable reasoning data to train LLMs in logical reasoning and beyond.

publications.name: On the Perception Bottleneck of VLMs for Chart Understanding
publications.summary: Investigation of the perception bottleneck in vision-language models for chart understanding.

publications.name: On the Universal Truthfulness Hyperplane Inside LLMs
publications.summary: Identification of a universal truthfulness hyperplane embedded within LLM internal representations.

publications.name: In-Context Sharpness as Alerts: An Inner Representation Perspective for Hallucination Mitigation
publications.summary: A framework for detecting and mitigating hallucinations in LLMs using in-context representation sharpness as a real-time alert mechanism.

publications.name: C-Eval: A Multi-Level Multi-Discipline Chinese Evaluation Suite for Foundation Models
publications.summary: A comprehensive multi-level, multi-discipline Chinese language benchmark for evaluating foundation model capabilities across various cognitive tasks.

publications.name: Composing Parameter-Efficient Modules with Arithmetic Operations
publications.summary: Novel approach to composing parameter-efficient modules via arithmetic operations.

projects.name: SynReason (SynLogic)
projects.description: Large-scale synthesis of verifiable reasoning data for training LLMs in logical reasoning tasks.

projects.name: Vision4Chart
projects.description: Improving chart understanding in vision-language models by addressing the perception bottleneck via contrastive learning.

projects.name: Universal Truthfulness Hyperplane
projects.description: Probing and mapping the internal truthfulness hyperplane in large language models.
